questions: Should non-admin users be able to interact with the bot via @mentions in public channels, or only via DMs? Public channel usage is visible to everyone and could be noisy.

When a non-admin user without `can_message_bot` messages the bot, should it silently ignore them or reply with a message (e.g., 'Ask an admin to enable bot access for you')? Silent ignore is cleaner but less discoverable.

Should `query_table` be available to members at all? It's read-only but exposes raw database queries. An alternative is to only expose the purpose-built search tools (find_messages, get_member_info, etc.) and remove query_table from the member tool set entirely.
Is there a need for a middle tier (e.g., moderators who can send_message but not delete_message), or is the binary admin/member split sufficient for now?
Should the `can_message_bot` flag be per-guild (on `guild_members`) or global (on `members`)? Per-guild is more flexible for multi-server but adds complexity.
success_criteria: Non-admin users with `can_message_bot=True` can DM the bot and receive responses using read-only tools

Non-admin users cannot invoke write tools (send_message, delete_message, edit_message, upload_file, share_to_social) — blocked at both prompt level (tools not offered to Claude) and executor level (rejected if somehow called)
Admin users experience zero regressions — all 16 tools remain available
Rate limiting prevents non-admin users from excessive API usage
search_logs and other sensitive read operations are restricted to admins
The `can_message_bot` flag can be toggled per-member via the database (and eventually via admin bot commands)
assumptions: Binary admin/member role split is sufficient (no moderator tier needed for v1)
The `can_message_bot` flag goes on the global `members` table, not per-guild `guild_members`, since multi-server bot access is not yet a real use case
Non-admin users should be able to interact via both DMs and @mentions (same channel detection logic as admin)
Rate limiting is in-memory only (resets on bot restart) — acceptable for v1
`query_table` will be available to members but with a restricted table subset (excluding system_logs, timed_mutes, invite_codes)
No new Discord slash commands needed — the existing message/mention flow is sufficient
Conversation history remains in-memory (no persistence change needed)
